title: Selling Tutorials, Ebooks, and Courses Without Gumroad or Payhip: What Actually Works When You Are in a Restricted Country
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: PayPal, Stripe, Gumroad, and Payhip do not work in your country. That is a platform problem, not a you problem. Here is what actually works.

content.text: Having a great digital product, such as an ebook, course, or tutorial, is only half the battle for creators in restricted countries, as collecting payment from customers can be a significant hurdle due to the limitations imposed by traditional payment platforms like PayPal, Stripe, Gumroad, and Payhip, leaving many to wonder how they can get paid online without these services, a problem that Unchained Commerce has solved for hundreds of creators, providing them with the tools they need to sell their products globally without the need for a PayPal or Stripe account
What You Are Actually Comparing

When comparing different platforms for selling digital products, it's essential to consider the underlying infrastructure and how it affects creators in restricted countries, where traditional payment processors like PayPal and Stripe are often not available, and even when they are, the KYC requirements and fraud risk tiers can make it difficult for creators to get paid, which is why crypto-native infrastructure is becoming an attractive alternative
Where the Traditional Platforms Break Down

The traditional platforms like Gumroad and Payhip break down for creators in restricted countries because they rely on traditional payment processors like PayPal and Stripe, which have strict compliance requirements and often exclude creators from certain countries, resulting in a significant barrier to entry for many digital creators, who are then forced to look for alternative solutions that can provide them with the same level of functionality and convenience
What Crypto-Native Infrastructure Does Differently

Crypto-native infrastructure, on the other hand, does not have the same geographic compliance layer as traditional payment processors, allowing creators in restricted countries to sell their digital products without the need for a PayPal or Stripe account, and with the help of platforms like Unchained Commerce, they can set up their own stores and start selling their products globally, with instant automated delivery and no subscription fees

The biggest hurdle for creators in restricted countries is not the quality of their products, but rather the ability to collect payment from customers, which is where crypto-native infrastructure can make a significant difference
The Real Cost Difference

The real cost difference between traditional platforms and crypto-native infrastructure lies not only in the transaction fees but also in the level of control and flexibility that creators have over their stores and products, with crypto-native infrastructure providing a more decentralized and autonomous solution that allows creators to keep more of their earnings and make decisions about their own businesses
Which One to Use and Why

For creators in restricted countries, crypto-native infrastructure is often the better choice because it provides a more reliable and accessible way to sell digital products, without the need for traditional payment processors or subscription fees, and with the help of platforms like Unchained Commerce, they can set up their own stores and start selling their products globally, with a high level of customization and control

For creators in Nigeria and other restricted countries, selling digital products without PayPal or Stripe is possible with the help of crypto-native infrastructure, which provides a more decentralized and autonomous solution that allows creators to keep more of their earnings and make decisions about their own businesses, and with platforms like Unchained Commerce, they can set up their own stores and start selling their products globally, with instant automated delivery and no subscription fees, making it the best PayPal alternative for creators in restricted countries

Crypto-native infrastructure provides a more reliable and accessible way to sell digital products, without the need for traditional payment processors or subscription fees, giving creators more control and flexibility over their businesses
